title: Phase 9 burrow ship V1 targets: fly + docker + tarball (not fly + render)
rationale: The second/third targets exist primarily to prove the ShipTarget interface is genuinely generic — a single implementation can't tell you whether fly-specific assumptions have leaked into the seam. Render was the original second target but the user does not use Render, so it would be untested code with weak dogfooding. fly + docker + tarball was chosen instead because: (1) tarball stresses interface SHAPE (sync, no network, no auth, no streaming) — catches leaky abstractions; (2) docker stresses interface LIFECYCLE (async, streaming build logs as events, long-running, can fail mid-stream) — catches event-model gaps; (3) fly is the real-world dogfood target and naturally COMPOSES the docker target (build image → push → flyctl deploy), so making docker a peer rather than fly-internal forces the docker-build primitive to be factored out cleanly. Docker is explicitly user-facing so 'burrow ship --target docker' is supported standalone (handing an image to a colleague, pushing to a private registry, running locally) — not just a means to fly. Fly credentials are NOT required to implement Phase 9; the target shells out to flyctl and assumes host-level auth, with --dry-run for validation without provider calls.
